title: canthium oligocarpum subsp. captum
intro: Canthium oligocarpum subsp. captum, also known as the Indian cigar plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Canthium oligocarpum subsp. captum include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and reduce fever, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, making it easily accessible for those seeking its medicinal benefits.
